prompt: List AI companies that became new unicorns ($1B+) or decacorns in 2026. For each: name, valuation, what they do, why they got there fast. Cite sources.
content:
**Several AI companies achieved new unicorn ($1B+) status in 2026 (primarily in early-to-mid year reports tracking “minted” unicorns), driven by the ongoing AI investment boom, star founding teams (often ex-DeepMind, OpenAI, Google, etc.), massive early funding rounds, and focus on high-demand areas like frontier models, AI infrastructure/hardware, and specialized applications.**[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)[[3]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/almost-40-new-unicorns-have-been-minted-so-far-this-year-here-they-are/)

No prominent *new* decacorns ($10B+) from 2026 launches appear in the results (higher valuations like those for established players such as OpenAI or xAI predate or exceed this timeframe). Here are key examples highlighted across sources as new 2026 unicorns, with available details on valuation, focus, and rapid rise (sourced primarily from contemporaneous reports on funding rounds and unicorn lists):

- **Ineffable Intelligence (UK)**: Reached ~$5.1 billion valuation (highest or among the highest new unicorns noted for 2026). It is a frontier AI lab building self-learning/reinforcement learning systems that learn through experience rather than relying primarily on human data, aiming for novel models that could outperform traditional large language models. Founded months earlier by ex-DeepMind researcher David Silver; it raised a record ~$1.1 billion seed round (Europe’s largest at the time) in April 2026, backed by Sequoia, Lightspeed, Nvidia, and Google. Speed came from the founder’s pedigree, a differentiated technical approach in a hot frontier AI space, and investor appetite for capital-intensive labs.[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)[[4]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/deepmind-ineffable-intelligence-record-seed-funding-nvidia-google.html)[[5]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/27/deepminds-david-silver-just-raised-1-1b-to-build-an-ai-that-learns-without-human-data/)

- **humans& (US, California)**: Reached ~$4.5 billion valuation. It develops “human-centric” AI systems/models emphasizing collaboration with people (rather than pure replacement or efficiency), founded by researchers from OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Google, and Meta. It raised a massive $480 million seed round in January 2026 (one of the largest seeds ever), backed by Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, SV Angel, GV, and Emerson Collective. The rapid unicorn status (from a company founded in late 2025) stemmed from an all-star team (“talent monopoly”), early unstructured funding at extreme scale, and positioning in the collaborative/enterprise AI wave.[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)[[6]](https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/humans-raises-huge-seed-round-unicorn-valuation/)[[7]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/20/humans-a-human-centric-ai-startup-founded-by-anthropic-xai-google-alums-raised-480m-seed-round/)

- **Ricursive Intelligence (also styled Recursive Intelligence; US)**: Reached ~$4 billion valuation. It is a frontier AI lab building AI-powered tools for chip design (and recursive self-improvement loops where AI designs better chips for training better AI), drawing on the team’s prior work on Google’s AlphaChip. Founded around late 2025/early 2026 by ex-Google researchers (e.g., Anna Goldie and Azalia Mirhoseini); it raised a $300+ million Series A (after a smaller seed) within months of launch, backed by Lightspeed and Sequoia. Speed resulted from proven technical pedigree in a critical AI bottleneck (hardware design timelines), addressing the compute demands of advancing models, and strong investor interest in infrastructure plays.[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)[[8]](https://www.ricursive.com/)[[9]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/startup-ai-lab-ricursive-seriesa-unicorn/)

- **Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI Labs; France)**: Reached ~$3.5 billion valuation. It is a core AI company (details on exact focus are less specified in overviews but grouped with infrastructure/foundation model plays). It appeared on lists of top new 2026 unicorns alongside other AI infrastructure bets. Rapid rise tied to the broader European/US investor push into scalable AI platforms.[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)

- **Waabi (Canada)**: Reached ~$3 billion valuation. It focuses on AI for autonomous vehicles/robotics (e.g., trucking or related systems). It was listed among highly valued new 2026 AI unicorns, benefiting from the robotics/autonomy tailwinds in AI funding.[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)

**Additional 2026 AI unicorns mentioned in TechCrunch-style trackers (often at lower $1–2B valuations) include Positron (AI semiconductors, ~$1B), Fundamental (foundational models for large datasets, ~$1.4B), Goodfire (AI interpretability/tools, ~$1.3B), and others like Arena or Flapping Airplanes.**[[3]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/almost-40-new-unicorns-have-been-minted-so-far-this-year-here-they-are/) Forbes’ 2026 AI 50 also noted newcomers such as Reflection (~$8B valuation in context, open-source models), Gamma (~$2.1B, AI presentation tools with strong revenue), and Chai Discovery (~$1.3B, AI drug discovery).[[10]](https://www.forbes.com/lists/ai50/)

**Overall context and caveats**: Reports indicate AI companies accounted for roughly 17–25+ of the ~70–98 new unicorns minted globally in the first half of 2026 (varying by source and cutoff date), concentrated in infrastructure, models, and vertical tools.[[1]](https://technext24.com/2026/04/26/70-unicorns-mint-in-2026-17-ai-startups/)[[2]](https://www.digitaljournal.com/business/ai-companies-account-for-over-a-quarter-of-2026s-98-newly-minted-unicorn-start-ups/article)[[11]](https://istart.com.au/news-items/ai-drives-2026-unicorn-surge-but-not-the-majority/) Valuations often came from large seed/Series A rounds amid hype, star talent, and strategic backing (e.g., Nvidia). Exact “new” status depends on precise timing of prior rounds; some may have had earlier lower valuations. Data is drawn from funding announcements and unicorn roundups as of mid-2026; the landscape evolves quickly. For the most current or exhaustive lists, refer to Crunchbase, PitchBook, or TechCrunch trackers.
